title: EU AI Act Compliance Checklist (2026 Standards)

requirements.title: Automated Record-Keeping and Traceability
requirements.description: The system must maintain detailed records of data sources, intermediate reasoning steps, and final actions, creating an audit trail showing exactly how the AI arrived at a decision.

evidence.description: Structured JSON logging configuration with fields: timestamp, session_id, tool_name, target_path, execution_time_ms, agent_role, workspace, hitl_gate, outcome
Splunk Universal Forwarder template monitoring /opt/openclaw/logs/*.json with sourcetype _json and index ai_agent_ops. Optional enterprise SIEM add-on.
LangfuseReporter wraps the Langfuse SDK for per-request agent traces. Activated via config/eval/langfuse.env. Primary observability backend.
Tails OpenCLAW gateway logs, pushes traces to Langfuse, evaluates real-time SOC alerts. Supports --env-file and --dry-run flags.

requirements.verification: logging.json parses correctly with all required fields. Langfuse telemetry pipeline verified via dry-run. Splunk template reviewed for path accuracy.

requirements.title: Human Oversight Mechanisms
requirements.description: The system must be designed with appropriate human supervision to prevent autonomous harm. State-mutating operations require explicit human approval.

evidence.description: HITL policy classifying 10 tools as autonomous (read-only) and 12 tools as requires_approval (mutative/destructive)
Architect agent system prompt enforces read-only mode, prohibits write_file, shell_exec, and all mutative tools
Executor agent system prompt mandates HITL gate checks before every action, halts on requires_approval classification

requirements.verification: permissions.json contains non-overlapping autonomous and requires_approval arrays. Both prompts reference the HITL gate protocol and permissions.json.

requirements.title: Risk Management and Quality Management
requirements.description: Regular audits of skill integrity and continuous monitoring of prompt injection vulnerability rates constitute a proactive risk management framework.

evidence.description: Safe skill installation wrapper that delegates to skillvet's safe-install.sh, running 48 vulnerability checks before any community skill installation
Executable wrapper (permissions 700) ensuring all future skill installations are routed through the security scanner
Alerting thresholds for prompt_injection_vuln_rate > 0.00, SOC risk indicators (chmod, /etc/, ~/.ssh/, canvas.eval)
AlertEvaluator engine with check_realtime() and check_periodic() methods, integrated into log-watcher.py for live SOC detection

requirements.verification: vet-install.sh exists with correct shebang and references safe-install.sh. alerts.json defines critical-severity alerts. AlertEvaluator passes all unit tests.

notes: All three EU AI Act requirements from SOP Section 6.3 have implementation evidence traceable to completed tasks. Status is 'partial' because: (1) Langfuse telemetry pipeline has been verified via dry-run but not yet connected with real credentials in production, (2) Splunk SIEM integration is template-only -- no forwarder deployed, (3) HITL enforcement is prompt-based and not yet validated under adversarial conditions. Upgrade to 'compliant' once Langfuse is live and at least one full audit cycle completes.
